reasoning:
The question asks if Trump will REFRAIN from announcing a next Fed Chair in 2025. This resolves to "No announcement by December 31" if true. 
Recent reporting (late October 2025) strongly points toward an announcement happening before the end of the year. The Treasury Secretary, Scott Bessent, has narrowed the shortlist to 5 candidates, plans to interview them after Thanksgiving, and intends to present them to Trump in December. Trump himself explicitly stated on Air Force One (Oct 27, 2025) that "a decision on the next Federal Reserve chair might be made by the end of the year." 
Weighing against an absolute certainty is the tightness of the schedule. Presenting finalists in December gives Trump a very short window (two to three weeks) to interview them and announce his choice before December 31. Additionally, Trump was quoted saying Powell will be "out of there in a few months," which technically aligns with early 2026 before Powell's May termination, though the context meant the search would end soon. Minor delays, further vetting, or political distractions could easily push a December announcement into January 2026. Therefore, while the administration intends to announce in late 2025 (making a "No" resolution highly probable), there remains a roughly 25% chance of slippage into early 2026. I am assigning a 25% probability to Trump REFRAINING from announcing the pick in 2025 (meaning a 75% chance he DOES announce).
